dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4750 IS: Semiconductor Superiority Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Budd (for himself and Mr. Bennet) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to clarify the application of the advanced manufacturing investment credit with respect to semiconductor manufacturing facilities located in outer space.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Semiconductor Superiority Act.

section.header: Clarifying application of advanced manufacturing investment credit for semiconductor manufacturing facilities located in outer space

subsection.text: Section 48D(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.header: Application to facilities located in outer space

subparagraph.text: In the case of an advanced manufacturing facility which is located in outer space—

clause.text: for purposes of paragraph (1), qualified property shall not fail to be treated as part of such facility solely because such qualified property is—

subclause.text: used to transport crew, goods, equipment, material, or supplies in outer space to and from such facility, or
not located in outer space, and
property shall not fail to be treated as qualified property solely because such property is located in outer space,
with respect to subparagraph (A)(iv), property shall not fail to be treated as integral to the operation of such facility solely because such property is—

item.text: used in the manner described in clause (i)(I), or
not located in outer space, and

subclause.text: with respect to subparagraph (B)(ii), functions related to manufacturing shall include—

item.text: transportation of crew, goods, equipment, material, or supplies to and from the facility.

subparagraph.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term outer space shall include low-Earth orbit.
For purposes of this subsection, the term qualified property shall not include a rocket or similar launch vehicle constructed for the purpose of propelling a payload from Earth into outer space.

subsection.text: Section 50(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (1)(B), by inserting or any qualified property which is part of an advanced manufacturing facility located in outer space (as such terms are defined under section 48D(b)) and held by a United States person if such property was launched from within the United States after section 168(g)(4), and

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (C), by striking and at the end,
in subparagraph (D), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and, and
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:
any qualified property which is part of an advanced manufacturing facility located in outer space (as such terms are defined under section 48D(b)).

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to property placed in service after the date of enactment of this Act.

Nothing in this Act, or the amendments made by this Act, shall be construed to create any inference with respect to the allowance or determination of the advanced manufacturing investment credit under section 48D of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 with respect to an advanced manufacturing facility located in outer space on or before the date of the enactment of this Act.
